name: Wales

seo.title: Wales travel, cities and events

seo.webpageDescription: Explore Wales with key facts, linked cities, events, food and travel context.

history.text: Gruffydd ap Llywelyn briefly united much of Wales under one ruler.
The Statute of Rhuddlan brought Wales under English crown administration.
The Laws in Wales Acts integrated Wales into the English legal system.
The National Assembly for Wales opened after devolution.
The legislature became known as Senedd Cymru, the Welsh Parliament.

worthSeeing.title: Snowdonia:
worthSeeing.text: Mountain routes, slate towns and national park scenery.

worthSeeing.title: Cardiff:
worthSeeing.text: Capital city, castle, bayfront and rugby weekends.

worthSeeing.title: Coast and castles:
worthSeeing.text: Pembrokeshire beaches, medieval castles and compact rail-road trips.

planningQuestions.a: Mountains, castles, Welsh language, rugby and rugged coastline.
English is widely used; Welsh is official and visible across the country.
